title: GHSA-j4fx-v3rq-7p8h Malicious npm Package Exfiltrates GitHub Copilot Authentication Tokens for AI Service Abuse
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-j4fx-v3rq-7p8h documents a malicious npm package that specifically targeted GitHub Copilot authentication token storage, stealing OAuth tokens used for Copilot AI code completion to enable unauthorized AI service usage at victim expense and access to private code submitted to Copilot for completion.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious PyPI Package Targets Anthropic Claude API Keys Stored in Developer Environment Files for AI Service Theft
description: Security researchers documented malicious PyPI packages specifically scanning for Anthropic Claude API keys in .env files, shell configuration files, and Python configuration modules, stealing the API keys to enable unauthorized Claude API usage at victim billing expense and access to sensitive prompts submitted through the stolen credentials.
label: AV-400

title: npm Package openai-unofficial Typosquats Official OpenAI npm Library Targeting AI Developer Credentials and API Keys
description: The malicious npm package openai-unofficial typosquatted the official OpenAI npm client library, targeting AI application developers integrating GPT and DALL-E APIs by stealing OpenAI API keys and organization IDs that provided access to expensive AI compute at victim expense.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious PyPI Package langchain-community-tools Typosquats LangChain Ecosystem to Steal All Configured LLM Provider API Keys
description: A malicious PyPI package named langchain-community-tools typosquatted the LangChain AI framework ecosystem, targeting AI developers building LLM applications by stealing all configured LLM provider API keys including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cohere, and Hugging Face tokens found in LangChain configuration files.
label: AV-200

title: GHSA-8x4p-r7v3-p9j7 Dependency Confusion Attack Targets Internal AI Model Serving Infrastructure Package Names Leaked via GitHub
description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-8x4p-r7v3-p9j7 documents a dependency confusion attack where an AI company's internal model serving infrastructure package names were leaked through public GitHub repositories, allowing attackers to register matching public packages that were automatically installed during internal ML platform builds.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in LangChain chain.py Configuration Alter LLM Provider Routing and Memory Backend Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in LangChain AI framework chain configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter LLM provider routing definitions and memory backend specifications, causing AI applications to route prompts to different language model providers and store conversation history in different backends from what developers configure.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious npm Package Steals GitHub Actions Workflow Dispatch Tokens Enabling Unauthorized Pipeline Triggering at Scale
description: A malicious npm package stole GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch tokens from CI environments, enabling attackers to trigger arbitrary workflow executions in compromised repositories, potentially causing resource exhaustion through unauthorized parallel workflow runs and enabling execution of sensitive deployment workflows without repository write access.
label: AV-410

title: PyPI Package autogen-studio-malicious Typosquats Microsoft AutoGen AI Framework Targeting Enterprise AI Developer Credentials
description: A malicious PyPI package typosquatted the Microsoft AutoGen multi-agent AI framework, targeting enterprise AI developers building autonomous agent systems by stealing Azure OpenAI Service credentials, Microsoft identity tokens, and AutoGen configuration files containing enterprise AI infrastructure access.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Microsoft Semantic Kernel AI orchestration configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter AI skill plugin registration paths and memory store backend configurations, causing AI orchestration systems to load different plugins and use different memory backends from what developers configure.

description: A malicious Rust crate typosquatted the Hugging Face candle machine learning framework for Rust, targeting developers building high-performance ML inference applications by stealing Hugging Face Hub tokens, model registry credentials, and cloud provider keys used for ML model deployment infrastructure.

description: GitHub Security Advisory GHSA-p4vw-7x3f-h2q9 documents a malicious npm package that poisoned Yarn's Plug'n'Play virtual filesystem cache, injecting malicious module resolutions that caused legitimate package import statements to resolve to attacker-controlled code without any modification to package.json or yarn.lock files.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Deepset Haystack AI search pipeline configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter pipeline component routing definitions and document retriever configurations, causing AI search systems to route queries through different components and retrieve documents from different sources from what developers configure.

description: A malicious PyPI package specifically targeted Weights and Biases wandb API keys stored in developer machine learning experiment tracking configurations, enabling attackers to access private ML experiments, steal trained model artifacts, and use the compromised API keys for unauthorized ML infrastructure access.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in LlamaIndex AI data framework configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter vector store connection settings and document loader authentication configurations, causing AI retrieval-augmented generation systems to store embeddings in different vector databases and authenticate to document sources differently.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in MLflow machine learning project configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter training script entry point definitions and hyperparameter specifications, causing MLflow to execute different training scripts with different parameters than what data scientists specify during experiment configuration.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Dagster data orchestration platform configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter asset materialization strategy definitions and IO manager configurations, causing data pipelines to materialize assets using different storage backends and IO strategies than what data engineers configure.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Apache Airflow configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter DAG discovery directory paths and authentication backend configurations, causing Airflow to load workflow definitions from different directories and authenticate users against different identity providers than what platform engineers configure.

description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Tecton enterprise ML feature platform configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter feature serving configuration and transformation function definitions, causing real-time ML feature pipelines to compute and serve different feature values than what data scientists configure during feature engineering.

title: LLM-Based Taxonomy-Aligned OSS Threat Detection Achieves 97 Percent Classification Accuracy Across Five Attack Vectors on 1000-Entry Dataset
description: A taxonomy-aligned large language model classification system demonstrated 97 percent accuracy in detecting and categorizing open source software supply chain threats across five attack vectors including AV-200 typosquatting, AV-300 Trojan Source, AV-400 malicious builds, AV-410 pipeline poisoning, and AV-509 dependency confusion on a curated dataset of 1000 verified real-world OSS security incidents.
label: AV-400
